QF_LIA/tightrhombus/tightrhombus-273-245.raw_description:
Benchmarks used to test cut selection engine in SMTInterpol.
Seem to be of interest because they're easy but many tools can't
solve them.

QF_LIA/arctic-matrix/constraint-415996.s.raw_description:
Submitted by Johannes Waldmann in 2012 for SMT-LIB.

These come from automated program termination analysis.
They should be satisfiable.

remove at least one strict rule from (RULES a# a -> b# b b,
       b# b -> c# c c,
       c# c -> d# d d,
       c# c -> d# d,
       b# -> d# d,
       d# d c -> a#,
       a a ->= b b b,
       a ->= d c,
       b b ->= c c c,
       c c ->= d d d,
       d e ->= e d c b a,
       b ->= d d,
       c e ->= e a a b,
       d d c ->= a)

QF_LIA/tropical-matrix/constraint-247831.raw_description:
Submitted by Johannes Waldmann in 2012 for SMT-LIB.

These come from automated program termination analysis.
They should be satisfiable.

remove at least one strict rule from (RULES a# a b b -> a# a a,
       a# a b b -> a# a,
       c# b -> c#,
       a a b b ->= b b b a a a,
       a c ->= c a,
       c b ->= b c)

       b# b b a b a b b a b -> b# b b a b a b b,
       b# b b a b a b b a b -> b# b a b a b b,

QF_LIA/wisa/wisa5.smt2.raw_description:
These benchmarks are formulas in quantifier-free Presburger arithmetic.  They
have been generated in software verification problems encountered by Vinod
Ganapathy and Somesh Jha in the Wisconsin Safety Analyzer (WiSA) project.
Please acknowledge the WiSA and UCLID projects in publications reporting
results on these benchmarks.  Thanks!

Sanjit Seshia, Feb. 2004

* WiSA project: http://www.cs.wisc.edu/wisa

These come from automated program termination analysis.
They should be satisfiable.

remove at least one strict rule from (RULES d# d a a -> b# b d d,
       d# d a a -> d# d,
       b# b d d b b -> c# c a a,
       c# c -> d# d,
       d d a a ->= b b d d,
       a a ->= b b b b b b,
       b b d d b b ->= c c a a,
       c c ->= d d)

QF_LIA/20230321-UltimateAutomizerSvcomp2.raw_description:
Generated by the tool Ultimate Automizer [1,2] which implements
an automata theoretic approach [3] to software verification.

This SMT script belongs to a set of SMT scripts that was generated by
applying Ultimate Automizer to benchmarks [4] from the SV-COMP 2023 [5,6].
This script may not contain all SMT commands that Ultimate Automizer
issued. In order to meet the restrictions for SMT-COMP benchmarks 
we dropped the commands for getting values (resp. models), 
unsatisfiable cores, and interpolants.

QF_LIA/20180326-Bromberger/more_slacked/.raw_description:
Publications: M. Bromberger. A Reduction from Unbounded Linear Mixed Arithmetic Problems into Bounded Problems. (Work in progress.)

The benchmarks in this class are based on the problems from SMT-LIB/QF_LIA/CAV_2009 and SMT-LIB/QF_LIA/cut_lemmas.
I just replaced all variables x with x_+ - x_-, where x_+ and x_- are two new variables such that x_+, x_- >= 0.
This transformation is equisatisfiable.
The new variables and an unsatisfiable status guarantee that there are bounded and unbounded directions.
Target solver: CVC4 Mathsat SPASS-IQ YICES Z3

The benchmarks in this class are based on the problems from SMT-LIB/QF_LIA/CAV_2009 and SMT-LIB/QF_LIA/cut_lemmas.
I just replaced all variables x with x_+ - x_-, where x_+ and x_- are two new variables such that x_+, x_- >= 0.
This transformation is equisatisfiable.
The new variables and an unsatisfiable status guarantee that there are bounded and unbounded directions.
Target solver: CVC4 Mathsat SPASS-IQ YICES Z3
QF_LIA/20180326-Bromberger/more_slacked/.description:
Application: This is a problem with unbounded directions and, therefore, a good test for the termination/completeness of branch-and-bound solvers.
Category: crafted
